subtitle: Deep hip pain, sometimes mimicking sciatica

strava_risk_triggers.risk_note: Piriformis tightness common in runners with weak glutes and high mileage

mild.description: Deep ache in the buttock, sometimes into the back of the thigh
mild.can_run_note: Yes — stretch thoroughly before and after
why: Piriformis overuse compensating for weak glute max and medius
Prolonged sitting compressing the piriformis against the sciatic nerve
Excessive hip internal rotation during running stride
relief: Figure-4 stretch: cross ankle over opposite knee, press knee away, hold 90s
Pigeon pose on the floor 2 min each side
Deep tissue massage with a ball on the buttock
Heat before stretching to soften the muscle
prevention: Glute strengthening to reduce piriformis overload
Avoid prolonged sitting — stand or walk every 45 minutes
Hip external rotator stretching as part of cool-down
Reduce hip internal rotation in gait if identified

moderate.description: Persistent deep buttock pain, shooting sensation down the back of the thigh
moderate.can_run_note: Easy flat runs only — stop if leg symptoms develop
why: Piriformis muscle impinging on the sciatic nerve — piriformis syndrome
Trigger points in the piriformis referring pain into the buttock and thigh
Hip rotator tightness from sustained mileage without adequate mobility work
relief: Daily piriformis stretching — minimum 3 × 2 min per side
Tennis ball deep tissue release on the buttock
prevention: Dry needling to piriformis trigger points can be effective

severe.description: Constant pain into the buttock and leg, numbness or tingling down the leg
severe.can_run_note: Do not run — possible sciatic nerve irritation
why: Significant piriformis syndrome with sciatic nerve compression
Lumbar spine involvement needs to be ruled out
Possible nerve sensitisation requiring specific management
relief: Physiotherapy assessment to differentiate piriformis syndrome from lumbar sciatica
Neural mobilisation techniques under physio guidance
Imaging if symptoms are severe or unresponsive
prevention: Treat root cause — differentiate lumbar vs piriformis before returning

see_physio_if: Numbness or tingling down the leg to the foot
Symptoms worse with sitting for more than 20 minutes
No improvement after 1 week of stretching
